title: Temporal
description: Durable execution platform that runs reliable, fault-tolerant workflows and activities as code at scale.
installation: This template deploys the PRIMARY Temporal server using the all-in-one `temporalio/auto-setup` image. On first boot auto-setup creates the database schema and starts all four server roles (frontend, history, matching, internal worker) in a single pod. PORTS: the server speaks gRPC on 7233 (the address SDK clients/workers connect to) and exposes an HTTP API on 7243 (the containerPort published here). Add-on: a Postgres add-on backs both the default and visibility keyspaces (DBNAME=temporal, VISIBILITY_DBNAME=temporal_visibility, auto-created by auto-setup). NOTE: auto-setup is the convenience bundle for self-hosting; Temporal recommends the separate `temporalio/server` + `temporalio/admin-tools` images with externally-managed schema for large production deployments, and advanced/large visibility setups can additionally use Elasticsearch or OpenSearch (set ENABLE_ES=true + ES_SEEDS) which is NOT provisioned here. COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as their own Kubero apps: (1) WEB UI -> image `temporalio/ui:latest` (listens on HTTP 8080), set TEMPORAL_ADDRESS=temporal-<pod/service>:7233 and TEMPORAL_CORS_ORIGINS={{KUBERO_APP_URL}}; this is the human-facing dashboard. (2) ADMIN/CLI -> image `temporalio/admin-tools:latest` for running `tctl`/`temporal` operator commands and manual schema upgrades. (3) Your own WORKERS are separate apps built from your code with the Temporal SDK, pointed at this server's 7233 gRPC endpoint. ROTATE the default Postgres password before going live. Set TEMPORAL_ADDRESS/namespaces via tctl after boot; a `default` namespace is auto-registered.
